<From Bulldog> "We do more health coverage than most of our competitors [Cosmopolitan, primarily]," she says. "We offer full-body health coverage, not just reproductive health or [female-focused topics like] breast cancer. We find that many statistics can be misleading, so we try to be aware of risk reality-check. It's best if you can put risks in perspective, and in the context of other health problems women face," Naugle says. "We also want our coverage to be unique, so don't offer me the same patient you've pitched to five other outlets." Naugle expects PR pros to have realistic coverage expectations: "Be willing to accept 'no' for an answer. Or, better yet, tell me in your pitch, 'I know this isn't a feature, but it's a perfect quickie.' We're always looking for [quick bits of info]. It's not products that lead to feature stories, it's ideas based on data with an interesting spin. Very quotable experts--funny and quirky--are also welcome. Tell me what sets him or her apart." Don't send photos; she arranges for a photo shoot if interested. Pitch via E-mail or mail.
